what_it_does: This file is a ledger record for a design-assets channel image asset named Icon_Gold_Convenient; it stores YAML metadata such as id, address, blob reference, MIME type, dimensions, version history, authorship, and timestamps, but contains no image data or descriptive alt text.
observations: Dimensions w and h are both 0
Alt text in version v1 is empty
created and updated timestamps are identical
No content appears after the YAML frontmatter
standouts: Zero width and height for an image asset record
Empty alt text on the only version
concerns: Zero dimensions suggest incomplete or placeholder metadata
Empty alt text limits accessibility and discoverability
No links or contextual usage information
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id/address/channel/path/blob/mime fields
Follows a versioned asset ledger entry pattern
Wrapped as a markdown file in channel-memory/images
notes: A sparse ledger stub for the gold 'convenient' icon asset with placeholder dimensions and no alt text.
questions: Why are the image dimensions recorded as 0?
What does the source value 'dragnet' indicate?
Where is the actual image content stored relative to the blob reference?
What alt text or usage label is intended for this icon?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose appears to be cataloging a design-assets icon; the actual content is metadata-only with zero dimensions and empty alt text, so it only partially fulfills that purpose.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to create a stable, addressable, versioned record for a design-assets icon so it can be referenced by id and blob address.
contribution: Provides a stable metadata record that links an icon name and id to a blob reference and a single version entry, supporting asset lookup and versioning in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory ledger file for the design-assets icon 'Icon_Gold_Convenient' containing YAML metadata, a blob reference, and a single version with zero dimensions and empty alt text.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and version a design-assets icon image so it can be referenced by id and blob address.
